Identification.LongTitle: An Act to enact the Climate-Aligned Finance Act and to make related amendments to other Acts

Provision.Text: Part 1 enacts the Climate-Aligned Finance Act which, among other things, establishes climate commitments and obligations of various entities in relation to them. Subsequent parts establish further obligations on entities â primarily financial entities â in relation to the climate commitments provided for under the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to enact the Climate Commitments Act and make related amendments to other Acts
An Act to require certain financial and other federally regulated entities to mitigate and adapt to the impacts of climate change

Heading.Label: PART 4
Heading.TitleText: Appointments, Conflicts of Interest and Duties

Text.XRefExternal: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act
Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act
Business Development Bank of Canada Act

Heading.TitleText: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act

Provision.Text: Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Enacting Climate Commitments Act.
The Climate-Aligned Finance Act is enacted as follows:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to require certain financial and other federally regulated entities to mitigate and adapt to the impacts of climate change

Provision.Text: Whereas there is a broad scientific consensus and high confidence that anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions cause global climate change and present an unprecedented risk to the environment â including its biological diversity â to human health and safety, to economic prosperity and to the stability of the Canadian financial system;

Whereas the impacts of climate change â such as coastal erosion, thawing permafrost, increases in heat waves, droughts and flooding â and related risks to critical infrastructure and food security are being felt throughout Canada and are impacting Canadians and disproportionately affecting Indigenous peoples, low-income citizens and northern, coastal and remote communities;
Whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes that it is the responsibility of the present generation to minimize the impacts of climate change on future generations;
Whereas the United Nations, Parliament and the scientific community have identified climate change as an issue of international concern that is unconstrained by geographic boundaries;

Whereas Canada has ratified the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, done in New York on May 9, 1992, and in force as of 1994, and the objective of that Convention is the stabilization of greenhouse gas concentrations in the atmosphere at a level that would prevent dangerous anthropogenic interference with the climate system;

Whereas Canada has ratified the Paris Agreement, done in Paris on December 12, 2015, and in force as of 2016, and the aims of that Agreement include holding the increase in the global average temperature to well below 2 degrees Celsius (2Â°C) above pre-industrial levels and pursuing efforts to limit the temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius (1.5Â°C) above pre-industrial levels, recognizing that this would significantly reduce the risks and impacts of climate change;

Whereas the Parliament of Canada adopted the Canadian Net-Zero Emissions Accountability Act, which requires a Government of Canada plan to achieve a prosperous net-zero-emissions future in Canada by 2050 at the latest, supported by public participation and expert advice;
Whereas the Parliament of Canada adopted the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act in furtherance of the "polluter pays" principle;

Whereas the Parliament of Canada adopted the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act to ensure that the laws of Canada are consistent with the Declaration and to implement an action plan to achieve its objectives;

Whereas our economic and financial systems are interdependent with life-supporting ecosystem services â such as a stable climate â making the maintenance of a stable climate a matter of superseding public interest;

Whereas climate-related financial risks cannot be treated as conventional financial risks because they are characterized by radical uncertainty and irreversible catastrophic consequences and therefore require a distinct approach to urgently align financial flows with climate commitments;

Whereas the Bank of Canada recognizes that climate change poses significant risks to the financial system and the economy, including physical risks that arise from more frequent and severe extreme weather events and risks that stem from the transition to a low-carbon, net-zero economy;

Whereas climate-related financial risk is endogenous to financial systems and continued financial support for emissions-intensive activities increases future climate-related risks to the stability of financial systems and the long-term interests of financial institutions;

And whereas investment in energy efficiency, clean energy and clean technologies and the incentivization of innovation and behavioural change must replace investments in greenhouse-gas-emission-intensive activities for effective action against climate change;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: climate change impact, in relation to an activity,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is positive if it furthers or facilitates either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: achievement of climate commitments, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an entityâs alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is negative if it makes achieving climate commitments less likely, including by contributing to an entity not being in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4. (effet sur les changements climatiques)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: obligations and commitments under the following, whether or not they are binding on an entity:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, done in New York on May 9, 1992, including the principles of common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities;

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Paris Agreement, done in Paris on December 12, 2015, and in force as of 2016;

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: amendments to the Paris Agreement made by the Conference of Parties under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, as well as any additional agreements or instruments adopted by the Conference of Parties after the entry into force of the Paris Agreement; and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the Canadian Net-Zero Emissions Accountability Act, including the national greenhouse gas emissions target of net-zero emissions by 2050 at the latest, as established under section 6 of that Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reduction of emissions on a pathway that respects the global carbon budget and is consistent with limiting global temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius (1.5Â°C) over pre-industrial levels, with no or low overshoot;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: elimination of dependence on and lock-in of emissions-intensive activities, including by avoiding new fossil fuel supply infrastructure and exploring for new fossil fuel reserves and instead planning for a fossil fuelâfree future;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: preservation, enhancement and restoration of natural carbon sinks, including forests and peatland; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: enhancement of the capacity to adapt and reduce vulnerability to actual and expected impacts of climate change, including by increasing the resilience of socio-economic, built and ecological systems. (engagements climatiques)

Definition.Text: emissions means all direct and indirect emissions of greenhouse gases set out in column 1 of Schedule 3 of the Greenhouse Gas Pollution Pricing Act that are associated with the full life cycle of an activity, including emissions

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: from all stages of production, from the point of resource extraction or utilization through to the completion of the activity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that occur after the activity, including emissions resulting from the distribution and end use of products resulting from the activity;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: associated with the activity that are scope 2 or scope 3 emissions as those concepts are described in guidance and standards contained in the Greenhouse Gas Protocol developed under the partnership of the World Resources Institute and the World Business Council for Sustainable Development, as updated from time to time; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: related to agriculture, forestry or other land-use change required for an activity. (Ã©missions)

Definition.Text: emissions-intensive activity means an activity that is a fossil fuel activity or that does one or more of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: produces emissions in a manner that does not align with climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: hampers the development or deployment of low-emission alternatives; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: leads to a lock-in of assets that are inconsistent with climate commitments. (activitÃ© Ã forte intensitÃ© dâÃ©missions)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Bank of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a bank, an authorized foreign bank or a bank holding company and its subsidiaries within the meaning of the Bank Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a body corporate to which the Trust and Loan Companies Act applies;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an association to which the Cooperative Credit Associations Act applies;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an insurance company or a fraternal benefit society incorporated or formed under the Insurance Companies Act;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a pension plan that has been registered under the Pooled Registered Pension Plans Act or the Pension Benefits Standards Act, 1985;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the Business Development Bank of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Infrastructure Bank;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the Canadian Commercial Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: Export Development Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: Farm Credit Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Development Investment Corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Pension Plan and the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board; and

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: the pension plans established under each of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Public Service Superannuation Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Royal Canadian Mounted Police Superannuation Act. (institution financiÃ¨re fÃ©dÃ©rale)

Definition.Text: financially facilitate means to provide any assistance or services of any financial value, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: funds or funding of any kind, including debt and equity financing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: project or general corporate financing of any kind;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: loans, loan guarantees, insurance or credit protection of any kind;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the issuance, purchase, transfer, securitization, arrangement, underwriting, syndication, sale or any other transaction of any security or derivative instrument; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: off-balance-sheet activities; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the provision of any advisory, consulting or management services. (faciliter financiÃ¨rement)

Definition.Text: fossil fuel activity means any activity involved in the fossil fuel supply or demand chain, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: exploring for or extracting, producing, exploiting, transporting, storing, exporting, refining or retailing oil, gas or coal; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the combustion of oil, gas or coal for energy generation in a power plant. (activitÃ© liÃ©e aux combustibles fossiles)

Definition.Text: global carbon budget means an amount calculated based on the best available science and precaution and equal to the maximum cumulative emissions into the atmosphere above which there is no longer a high probability of limiting global temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius (1.5Â°C) above pre-industrial levels. (budget carbone mondial)
person with climate expertise means a person with demonstrable experience in proposing or implementing climate actions and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: who has expertise in or knowledge of one or more of the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: climate change science, including the environmental, ecological, social, economic and distributional effects of climate change,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: physical and social sciences experience â such as scenario analysis â or knowledge â such as knowledge of carbon sinks â that is relevant to climate change,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: climate change and climate policy at the international, national or subnational levels, including the likely effects and efficacy of potential responses to climate change,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: social innovation and technologies that contribute to decarbonization,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: science-based energy supply-and-demand scenarios that are consistent with climate commitments, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: Indigenous ways of knowing, being and doing; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: who has acute lived experience related to the physical or economic damages of climate change.

ContinuedDefinition.Text: It does not include a person who is described in any of paragraphs 13(1)(a) to (d). (personne ayant une expertise en matiÃ¨re de climat)

Definition.Text: reporting entity means any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a federal financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation within the meaning of the Canada Business Corporations Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a work, undertaking or business within the legislative authority of Parliament that is described in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) or (j) of the definition federal work, undertaking or business in section 2 of the Canada Labour Code; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an entity listed in schedule III of the Financial Administration Act. (entitÃ© dÃ©clarante)

Subsection.Text: The purposes of this Act are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to align the activities of reporting entities with the public interest objective of achieving climate commitments; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to address systemic risks related to climate change.

Subsection.Text: To achieve its purposes, this Act limits the risks that financial institutions pose to the climate and the financial risks that climate change poses to the Canadian financial system. It does so by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: setting baseline requirements for entities to achieve climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: providing greater certainty and transparency regarding entitiesâ respect for their responsibility relative to the global carbon budget;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: ensuring that appropriate oversight and capital adequacy requirements are developed by supervisory and regulatory authorities;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: requiring directors, officers and administrators to align entities with climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: promoting timely and meaningful progress towards the alignment of entities with the stability of both the financial system and climate in relation to systemic risks posed by emissions-intensive activities;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: ensuring climate expertise on certain boards and prohibiting certain conflicts of interest; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: requiring the development of action plans, targets and progress reports for entities in respect of climate commitments through reporting requirements.

Subsection.Text: An entity in alignment with climate commitments

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: substantially contributes to realizing climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: makes no decision or determination that facilitates, furthers or financially facilitates another person or entity acting in a way that is inconsistent with climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: does not cause, exacerbate or prolong vulnerabilities to the effects of climate change, including loss of biodiversity;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: refrains from any land disturbance â including destruction or degradation of forests and peatland â affecting carbon sinks unless the projectâs or activityâs end state will result in a positive climate change impact;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: produces overall positive or neutral climate change impacts; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: does not undermine legal and other remedies available to redress climate harm or negative climate change impacts.

Subsection.Text: An entity that is in alignment with climate commitments

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respects the rights of Indigenous Peoples, including those rights enshrined in the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, which was adopted by the General Assembly of the United Nations as General Assembly Resolution 61/295 on September 13, 2007; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the context of its climate-related actions,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: takes into consideration vulnerable groups, communities and ecosystems, including the biodiversity of those ecosystems,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: makes decisions based on equity and the best available science and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: does not promote, foster or exacerbate food insecurity or inequalities in society; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: does not cause significant harm to social and environmental obligations recognized by Canada.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: climate commitments alignment report means a report that, in relation to an entity,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: demonstrates, with reference to the best available scientific evidence, how the entity is aligned with climate commitments as described in section 4;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: includes

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: details of the entityâs targets with respect to climate commitments and sets out specifically how they align with a pathway that respects the global carbon budget and is consistent with limiting global temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius (1.5Â°C) over pre-industrial levels, with no or low overshoot,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the entityâs plans to reach the targets provided in subparagraph (i),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: details on the entityâs progress on achieving targets and implementing its plans,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a description of how the entityâs targets and plans with respect to climate commitments represent responsible stewardship of an equitable allocation of the global carbon budget based on the entityâs historic emissions and the different development needs of regions and communities,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: details of the material assumptions made by the entity in relation to climate commitments, including those related to economic life and contingent liabilities related to emissions-intensive assets,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: details on the entityâs emissions, specifying in particular the sources of information used to calculate the emissions, any assumptions, the methods used to verify the calculations, and any other relevant information with respect to the quality of the information being reported by the entity in respect of its emissions, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: information regarding any activity undertaken either by the entity or on its behalf that is an activity described in paragraphs 5(1)(a) or (b) of the Lobbying Act â if a reference in those paragraphs to âpublic officer holderâ is read as a reference to âpublic office holder, public body, partnership, trade union or associationâ â on a matter related to the environment or climate; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: discloses for each of the entityâs directors, officers and administrators their involvement in any activity described in paragraphs 13(1)(a) to (d) and provides details in relation to that involvement. (rapport sur lâalignement sur les engagements climatiques )

Definition.Text: plans means a document developed by an entity in accordance with section 6 that details how the entity will achieve its targets so that it is in alignment with climate commitments. (plans)
targets means the goals set by an entity in accordance with section 6 for the reduction of its emissions so that it is in alignment with climate commitments

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for each of the years 2025, 2030, 2035, 2040, 2045 and 2050; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for any other year for which it establishes a target. (cibles)

Subsection.Text: An entity must develop its plans and targets with a view toward aligning its actions with climate commitments as soon as possible.
Plans must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: measures to prioritize and encourage

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: immediate and ambitious action,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: emissions reductions within the value chain, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: change and innovation to replace emissions-intensive activities;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: measures on operational and capital allocation for existing and new lines of business to ensure the achievement of targets; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: consideration of how the entityâs executive compensation, governance and strategy can be deployed to achieve targets.

Subsection.Text: Plans and targets cannot

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: allow for offsets to be used as a replacement for reducing emissions unless

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the entity has undertaken the maximum feasible mitigation of emissions,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the offsets are produced through proven emissions removal methods, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the offsets are strictly necessary to neutralize minimal residual emissions that cannot be abated with available technology; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: rely on or presuppose the future invention, discovery or large-scale deployment beyond the remit of the entityâs activities of any emissions removal, capture or storage technology, tool or technique to claim or promise reductions in emissions for the purpose of justifying continued or increased fossil fuel activities.

Subsection.Text: If plans or targets make use of offsets as permitted under paragraph (3)(a), they must include detailed information that sets out and explains the quality, certification and third-party assurance verification used to justify reliance on offsets and how the use of those offsets aligns with climate commitments.

If plans or targets rely on or assume the future invention, discovery or large-scale deployment beyond the remit of the entityâs activities of emissions removal, capture or storage within the limit of paragraph (3)(b), the entity must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contribute directly to making such future invention, discovery or large-scale deployment feasible and available within the relevant time frame; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: include detailed information, including an economic analysis, on how the reliance or assumption

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: satisfies the requirement of paragraphs (3)(b) and (5)(a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: aligns with climate commitments.

Subsection.Text: In respect of a federal financial institution,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: emissions, for the purposes of developing plans and targets, include emissions that are facilitated or furthered by the entity, including by being financially facilitated by it;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: targets must include absolute emissions targets established at the sectoral and portfolio level and for individual investment holdings across all classes of debt and equity; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: plans must include details of how and to what extent the institution engages with entities it financially facilitates to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: incentivize decommissioning emissions-intensive activities, diversifying energy sources, financing zero-emissions energy and infrastructure, and developing and adopting change and innovation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: escalate climate concerns regarding emissions-intensive activities of financially facilitated entities and exclude entities that are unable or unwilling to align with climate commitments, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: minimize actions that have a negative climate change impact, if any.

Subsection.Text: No later than 60 days after the end of each financial year, a reporting entity must prepare and make available to the public a climate commitments alignment report, unless it can demonstrate that it had no or negligible emissions in the preceding financial year.

For the purposes of subsection (1), an entity makes a climate commitments alignment report available to the public by making it easily accessible from the public-facing website of the entity to which it relates and by ensuring that it can be viewed free of charge and without registration of any kind.

If an enactment requires a reporting entity to provide an annual report or financial statements to any person or entity at a particular time or at particular intervals â including at an annual general meeting â the reporting entityâs most recently completed climate commitments alignment report must form part of those financial statements despite any provision of any other enactment.

Section.Text: The Minister designated under section 5 of the Canadian Net-Zero Emissions Accountability Act may, on recommendation of the Net-Zero Advisory Body and based on the best available international standards and science,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: develop and provide any tool, form or guidance on which a reporting entity may rely when preparing its climate commitments alignment report, plans or targets;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: adapt any tool, form or guidance developed under paragraph (a) to a specific sector or type of enterprise, such as small businesses or other small-scale entities; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: by order, define ânegligible emissionsâ for the purposes of subsection 7(1).

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent of Financial Institutions must develop guidelines for capital adequacy for a bank, an authorized foreign bank or a bank holding company and its subsidiaries within the meaning of the Bank Act, and those guidelines must account for exposures and contributions to climate-related risks and include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: increased capital-risk weights for financing exposed to acute transition risks, considering

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a risk weight of 1,250% for any loan, bond or derivative exposure to new fossil fuel resources or infrastructure,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: increasing risk weights to 150% or more for any loan, bond or derivative exposure to any fossil fuel activity,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: differentiation in transition-risk intensity among oil, gas and coal exposures, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the existence of short-term climate action plans aligned with climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a systemic climate risk-contribution capital surcharge that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: recognizes the extent to which the activities of financial institutions financially facilitate emissions,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: bolsters resilience in the face of systemic risks being contributed to through financially facilitating emissions-intensive activities, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: uses an institutionâs level of financially facilitated emissions as a proxy for its contribution to the systemic risk it places on the financial system; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other microprudential and macroprudential measures aimed at ensuring that financial institutions are in alignment with climate commitments.

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent of Financial Institutions must develop guidelines for funding requirements in respect of climate commitments for the entities listed in paragraphs (b) to (f) of the definition federal financial institutions. These guidelines must encompass all asset classes and types of activities that those federal financial institutions undertake to financially facilitate emissions-intensive activities.

The guidelines developed under subsection (1) must be published on an easily accessible public-facing portion of the Superintendentâs website no later than one year after the day on which this section comes into force.

The guidelines developed under subsection (2) must be published on the Superintendentâs website no later than six months after the day on which the guidelines developed under subsection (1) were published on that website.
The Superintendent

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must use the guidelines developed under this section to assess whether an entity over which the Superintendent has supervisory authority maintains adequate capital as required by any enactment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must develop and apply enforcement measures, within the Superintendentâs existing powers, duties and functions, that are aimed at ensuring compliance with the guidelines developed under this section.

Section.Text: The Minister of Finance and the President of the Treasury Board must study the guidelines developed under subsections 9(1) and (2), and the Minister of Finance must cause to be tabled before both Houses of Parliament, no later than six months after the day on which the guidelines developed under subsection 9(4) were published, a report that contains

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: guidelines for adequate capital, budgets or funding in respect of climate commitments for any entity that is subject to any provision of the Financial Administration Act but does not report to the Superintendent of Financial Institutions; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an action plan to make the guidelines referred to in paragraph (a) legally binding including the list of legislative amendments that the Government of Canada intends to introduce for that purpose.

Heading.Label: PART 4
Heading.TitleText: Appointments, Conflicts of Interest and Duties

Section.Text: In this Part, organization has the meaning assigned by section 2 of the Lobbying Act.
At all times, at least one member of the board appointed under each of the following provisions must be a person with climate expertise:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 5 of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 6 of the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 3.1 of the Canadian Commercial Corporation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: sections 4 and 5 of the Export Development Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 105 of the Financial Administration Act, unless that board is in respect of an entity that does not need to develop a climate commitments alignment report because it had no or negligible emissions in the previous financial year;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 5 of the Farm Credit Canada Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 6 of the Business Development Bank of Canada Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: section 9 of the Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: section 8 of the Canada Infrastructure Bank Act.

Subsection.Text: No person may be appointed to the board of a reporting entity if that person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: controls any capital, shares, stock, voting power or voting rights or has any ownership or legal interest in an organization that does not meet the description of an entity in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4, except to the extent that it is an asset described in paragraphs (b) to (m) of the definition exempt asset in section 20 of the Conflict of Interest Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: occupies any position in an organization that is not in alignment with climate commitments unless the sole purpose of that position is to assist the organization in meeting the description of an entity in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: has, in the last five years, engaged in any activity described in paragraphs 5(1)(a) or (b) of the Lobbying Act on behalf of any organization that is not aligned with climate commitments as described in section 4; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provides any services to an organization that does not meet the description of an entity that is not aligned with climate commitments as described in section 4 unless those services are for the sole purpose of enabling that organization to be an entity aligned with climate commitments as described in section 4.

Subsection.Text: An appointment made in contravention of subsection (1) is void from the time it is made.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to appointments made after the third anniversary of the day on which this section comes into force.

Section.Text: For greater certainty, sections 12 and 13 apply in addition to any obligations in respect of appointments made under any enactment.

Subsection.Text: No person appointed under any provision listed in paragraphs 12(a) to (i) may accept any gift, benefit or contribution of any value or kind from or on behalf of an organization that is not in alignment with climate commitments.
Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of any amount owed to a person by court order or by operation of law.

When acting in their official capacities, directors, officers or administrators of reporting entities have a duty to exercise their powers and functions in a way that enables the entity for which they are officers, directors or administrators to be in alignment with climate commitments.

The persons for whom a duty is established under subsection (1) must give precedence to that duty over all other duties and obligations of office, and, for that purpose, ensuring the entity is in alignment with climate commitments is deemed to be a superseding matter of public interest. However, this precedence does not supersede any requirement under the Income Tax Act or regulations made under that Act, nor does this precedence affect the eligibility of pension schemes under the Income Tax Act or its regulations.

Despite any provision of any other enactment, the Superintendent of Financial Institutions may issue any order they consider appropriate to any entity listed in paragraphs (b) to (f) of the definition federal financial institutions if, in their opinion, doing so is in alignment with climate commitments or will help the entity to which the order is issued ensure that it is in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4.

If authorized by an enactment, the Superintendent may issue a direction or order to any entity over which they have supervisory jurisdiction, for the purpose of facilitating that entityâs respect for any guideline prepared by the Superintendent under this Act.

In consultation with other relevant ministers of the Crown, the responsible Minister must prepare an action plan to incentivize financial products that support climate commitments and disincentivize those that are inconsistent with climate commitments, with a view to identifying legislative amendments to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: taxation legislation, including exemptions under taxation legislation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Schedule III to the Pension Benefits Standards Regulations, to prohibit investments that are inconsistent with climate commitments;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: legislation governing bankruptcy proceedings so as to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: prioritize the repayment of bonds whose purpose is to support climate commitments, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: disincentivize the repayment of bonds whose purposes are inconsistent with climate commitments; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: enact appropriate enforcement mechanisms, including through the enactment of criminal offences for making false or misleading statements in climate commitment alignment reports.

Subsection.Text: In developing the action plan, the responsible minister must collaborate with provinces to encourage alignment with climate commitments throughout the entire Canadian financial and commercial system, including in relation to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: securities disclosure for entities incorporated or regulated under the law of a province or territory, in order to ensure transparency and clarity of disclosure; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: protecting consumers from misleading information regarding climate commitment alignment reports, including through enacting related whistleblower protections.

Subsection.Text: In developing the action plan, the responsible minister must also consult with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent of Bankruptcy appointed under subsection 5(1) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent of Financial Institutions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Bank of Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: persons with climate expertise.

Subsection.Text: The action plan must provide

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: criteria for identifying financial products whose purposes are aligned with climate commitments, taking into account this Act and the best available international standards and science and justifying any departure from them;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: mechanisms to prevent the proceeds of financial products whose purposes are aligned with climate commitments from being used for activities that are inconsistent with climate commitments; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a description of legislative amendments to federal laws â including the Income Tax Act and Regulations under that Act, the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, the Companiesâ Creditors Arrangement Act and Schedule III to the Pension Benefits Standards Regulations â to incentivize financial products that support climate commitments and to disincentivize the use of products that are inconsistent with climate commitments.

Subsection.Text: In this section, responsible minister means the person designated under subsection (6).
The Governor in Council may, by order, designate a member of the Queenâs Privy Council for Canada as the responsible minister for this section.
A report containing the action plan must be completed no later than one year after the day on which this section comes into force.
No later than 20 sitting days after receiving the report completed under subsection (4), the responsible minister must cause the report to be tabled in both Houses of Parliament, along with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a detailed analysis of the legislative amendments contained in the action plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a proposal, including a timetable, for making the legislative amendments included in the action plan.

Section.Text: The preamble to the Bank of Canada Act is amended by adding the following after the first paragraph:

Provision.Text: And whereas the Bank of Canada must act in alignment with climate commitments;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 18:

The Bank may only exercise its powers under this Act in a way that permits it to be an entity that is in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10.1(1) of the Export Development Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: whether the project is likely to have adverse environmental effects or a negative climate change impact â as that term is defined in section 2 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act â despite the implementation of mitigation measures; and

Subsection.Text: Before entering, in the exercise of its powers under subsection 10(1.1), into a transaction that is related to a project, the Corporation must determine, in accordance with the directive referred to in subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the project is likely to have adverse environmental effects despite the implementation of mitigation measures; and

Subsection.Text: Section 10.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

A directive may only be issued under subsection (2) if it enables the entities involved in the project to be entities that are in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Acts.

The Corporation must, as soon as practicable, revise any directive issued under subsection (2) â including those issued before the coming into force of paragraph (2.1) â with a view to mitigating any negative climate change impacts as defined in section 2 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10.1:
The Corporation may only exercise its powers in a way that enables it to be an entity that is in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.
Subsection 89(3) of the Financial Administration Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (1) and (2), the Governor in Council is deemed to have given each parent Crown corporation listed in schedule III a directive to be an entity that is in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.
[Repealed, 1991, c. 24, s. 23]

Heading.TitleText: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: The Office must pursue its objects in a way that enables it and every entity it regulates or supervises to be in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 8:

Section.Text: Subsection 6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Duties, powers and functions of the Superintendent

Subsection.Text: The Superintendent has the powers, duties and functions assigned to them by the Acts referred to in the schedule to this Part â and the Climate-Aligned Finance Act â and shall examine into and report to the Minister from time to time on all matters connected with the administration of the provisions of those Acts except those that are consumer provisions as defined in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act.
The Superintendent must exercise their powers, duties and functions in a way that aligns with climate commitments as that term is defined in section 2 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

The Superintendent has the powers, duties and functions assigned to the Superintendent by the Acts referred to in the schedule to this Part and shall examine into and report to the Minister from time to time on all matters connected with the administration of the provisions of those Acts except those that are consumer provisions as defined in section 2 of the Financial Consumer Agency of Canada Act.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph 38(a) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of ensuring that financial institutions, when engaging in activities described in paragraphs 6(2)(a) to (c), are in alignment with climate commitments as that term is defined in section 2 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act; and

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 10:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portions of section 38:

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that is required or authorized by this Act to be prescribed; and

Heading.TitleText: Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act

Section.Text: The Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act is amended by adding the following after section 5:

The Board may only exercise its powers and carry out its objects so that it and the pension plans for which it invests are in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Public Sector Pension Investment Board Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 11:

Heading.TitleText: Business Development Bank of Canada Act

Section.Text: The Business Development Bank of Canada Act is amended by adding the following after section 22:
The Bank may only exercise its powers in a way that enables it to be in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Business Development Bank of Canada Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 12:

Section.Text: Section 7 of the Canada Infrastructure Bank Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The Board may only exercise its powers in a way that enables it and the Bank to each be an entity that is in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

Section.Text: Section 29 of the Canadian Net-Zero Emissions Accountability Act is replaced by the following:

Section 23 comes into force on the day on which a bill entitled An Act to enact the Climate-Aligned Finance Act and to make related amendments to other Acts, introduced in the 1st session of the 44th Parliament, receives royal assent.
Section 23 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Subsection.Text: In this Part, climate commitments and persons with climate expertise have the meanings assigned by section 2 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.
For the purposes of this Part, the provisions enacted by this Act

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: do not include any provision enacted by this Act that is repealed or spent at the time the review is undertaken; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: include any provision enacted by this Act that is subsequently modified except by way of repeal.

Subsection.Text: Within two years after the day on which this Act receives royal assent, the Minister of Finance must cause to be tabled in each House of Parliament

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a report regarding Indigenous Peoplesâ perspectives on the activities of the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions and the Bank of Canada â including in respect of long-term investments, adaptation and resilience of the Office and the Bank within the financial system and the stewardship of the financial system by the Office and the Bank for future generations â that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: co-developed with the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions, the Bank of Canada and representatives of Indigenous Peoples, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: based on consultations with Indigenous Peoples on these matters;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a report prepared by the Bank of Canada and developed in consultation with persons with climate expertise that evaluates whether monetary policy established by the Bank aligns with climate commitments described in section 2 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act and makes recommendations to entities' being in alignment with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

Subsection.Text: The reports described in paragraphs (1)(a) and (b) must include details on the consultations and co-development that occurred in their preparation.

The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any federal minister to be the Minister responsible for this Act, and that Minister must cause to be conducted, every three years beginning on the day on which this section comes into force, an independent review of the provisions enacted by this Act and their administration.

The Minister referred to in subsection (1) must cause a report on the independent review conducted under that subsection to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the review is completed, which must be no later than nine months after the review was commenced.

Section.Text: In every third year after the year in which this section comes into force, a comprehensive review of the provisions enacted by this Act and their operation is to be undertaken and completed by a committee of the Senate, of the House of Commons or of both Houses of Parliament that is designated or established for that purpose.

Subsection.Text: Every year after this section comes into force, a report on the implementation of the provisions enacted by this Act must be completed by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Superintendent of Financial Institutions, in respect of entities over which the Superintendent has supervisory oversight; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister of Finance, in respect of parent Crown corporations as defined under subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act.

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Finance must cause any report developed under subsection (1) to be laid before both Houses of Parliament no later than 10 sitting days after the report is completed.

Section.Text: Section 12 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: section 10 of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act;

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 20 :

Heading.TitleText: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act

Section.Text: Section 5 of the Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act is renumbered as subsection 5(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: The Board may only carry out its objects in a way that ensures that both it and the Canada Pension Plan align with climate commitments as described in section 4 of the Climate-Aligned Finance Act.

ExplanatoryNote.TitleText: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board Act
ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 21:

Subsection.Text: The provisions of this Act, other than Part 4, come into force on the first anniversary of the day on which it receives royal assent.

Part 4 comes into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that order may not be made and shall not in any case have any force or effect unless the lieutenant governor in council of each of at least two-thirds of the included provinces â within the meaning of subsection 114(1) of the Canada Pension Plan â having in the aggregate not less than two-thirds of the population of all of the included provinces, has signified the consent of that province to the enactment.
